name: API Endpoint Rate Limit Baseline
objective: Apply a tiered rate limiting baseline to all /api/* paths: 100 req/min for unauthenticated requests, 500 req/min for authenticated requests (JWT bearer detected), with burst allowance
risk_mitigated: API abuse, scraping, DDoS via API endpoint flooding, excessive data harvesting
potential_side_effects: Batch processing jobs or high-throughput integrations may hit the unauthenticated rate limit. Ensure all machine-to-machine clients use proper Authorization headers so they benefit from the higher authenticated threshold.
tuning_notes: Adjust thresholds based on observed p99 request rates in your API gateway logs before deploying. Consider per-endpoint limits for particularly sensitive or expensive endpoints (e.g., /api/export, /api/search). Use Cloudflare's custom response to return a Retry-After header.
deployment_notes: Run in 'log' mode for one week, collecting p50/p95/p99 request rates per endpoint and IP. Use this data to set appropriate thresholds. Ensure your application teams know the limits and that SDKs/clients implement exponential backoff.
monitoring_notes: Track rate limit hits by endpoint path and client ASN. High hit rates on /api/search or /api/export may indicate scraping. Consider serving challenge pages instead of hard blocks for ambiguous traffic.

unauthenticated.description: API unauthenticated rate limit — k1N pack v1.0

response.body: {"error": "Rate limit exceeded. Provide an Authorization header for higher limits."}

authenticated.description: API authenticated rate limit — k1N pack v1.0

response.body: {"error": "Rate limit exceeded. Please back off and retry with exponential backoff."}
